isCompliant(address).details: For FCA compliance, this is PUBLIC verification only.      Full records are maintained off-chain for regulatory disclosure.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

paused().details: Returns true if the contract is paused, and false otherwise.

proxiableUUID().details: Implementation of the ERC1822 {proxiableUUID} function. This returns the storage slot used by the implementation. It is used to validate the implementation's compatibility when performing an upgrade. IMPORTANT: A proxy pointing at a proxiable contract should not be considered proxiable itself, because this risks bricking a proxy that upgrades to it, by delegating to itself until out of gas. Thus it is critical that this function revert if invoked through a proxy. This is guaranteed by the `notDelegated` modifier.

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

setVerifyingKey(uint8,uint256[2],uint256.details: Called after trusted setup ceremony completes

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

upgradeTo(address).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

upgradeToAndCall(address,bytes).details: Upgrade the implementation of the proxy to `newImplementation`, and subsequently execute the function call encoded in `data`. Calls {_authorizeUpgrade}. Emits an {Upgraded} event.

params.proofType: The type of proof being verified
params.publicInputs: The public inputs to the circuit

returns.proofHash: The hash of the verified proof

addressProofs(address,uint8).notice: Valid proofs for an address (address => proofType => proofHash)

authorizedOracles(address).notice: Authorized oracles that can update sanctions list

hasValidProof(address,uint8).notice: Check if an address has a valid proof of a specific type

isCompliant(address).notice: Check if address is compliant (has all required proofs)

revokeProof(bytes32,string).notice: Revoke a proof (e.g., if sanctions list updated)

sanctionsListRoot().notice: Sanctions list Merkle root (updated by oracle)

setVerifyingKey(uint8,uint256[2],uint256.notice: Set verification key for a proof type

verify(uint8,(uint256[2],uint256[2][2],u.notice: Verify a proof without storing (view function for off-chain checks)

verifyAndStore(uint8,(uint256[2],uint256.notice: Verify a Groth16 proof and store the result

verifyingKeySet(uint8).notice: Whether a verification key has been set
